children.name: Struthio
children.description: The only extant genus in the family, comprising the largest living birds on Earth. Flightless and powerfully built, they are adapted for high-speed running across African savannas.

children.name: Struthio camelus
children.description: The largest living bird, reaching heights of up to 2.8 metres and weights over 150 kilograms. Males are unmistakable with black-and-white plumage and a bare pink neck, while females are brownish-grey. Found across open savanna and Sahel regions of Africa, they are powerful runners reaching speeds of 70 km/h.

children.name: Struthio molybdophanes
children.description: Formerly considered a subspecies of the Common Ostrich but now recognised as a distinct species. It has a bluish-grey neck and thighs and prefers the arid bushlands and deserts of the Horn of Africa. Slightly smaller than its cousin, it shares the same impressive running capabilities.
